title: Interactive Food Web Builder
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Build a temperate forest food web by connecting organisms with feeding relationships, then test trophic cascades by removing species.

learningObjectives: Construct a food web showing feeding relationships among organisms in a temperate forest ecosystem
Predict the effects of removing a species on other populations in the food web
Explain why biodiversity and interconnectedness contribute to ecosystem stability
prerequisites: Understanding of producers, consumers, and decomposers
Familiarity with energy transfer between trophic levels

keyQuestions: What happens to other species when a top predator is removed from the food web?
Which species in this web might be considered a keystone species and why?
How does the number of connections an organism has relate to its importance in the ecosystem?
Why do trophic cascades affect species that are not directly connected to the removed organism?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think food webs are simple linear chains rather than complex interconnected networks
Many students believe removing a top predator has no effect on producers
Students may think decomposers are unimportant because they are at the bottom of the web
assessmentOpportunities: Evaluate the accuracy of student-constructed food webs before checking
Assess written predictions about species removal cascades against simulation results
Have students identify the keystone species and justify their choice with evidence from the simulation
